title: Two attempts, two lessons
body: My first attempt taught me the syllabus. My second taught me time management under pressure. If this is your first attempt, don't aim for perfection — aim for full syllabus coverage with timed practice.

title: Don't skip the 'boring' core
body: Discrete Maths, Digital Logic, and COA feel dry but show up everywhere indirectly. Build these early so later subjects like OS and CN make intuitive sense.

title: Mock tests are non-negotiable
body: From three months out, take at least one full-length mock every week. The score matters less than learning to triage — knowing which questions to skip and return to.

title: A re-attempt is not a failure
body: I qualified in both 2025 and 2026. The second attempt, used well, can get you into a significantly better program than the first. Don't let one result define the plan.

title: Short notes over long notes
body: In the last 30 days, you won't re-read full textbooks. Keep a single-page formula and concept sheet per subject, built as you study — not after.

title: Ask early, ask specific
body: Vague questions like 'how to prepare' are hard to answer well. Bring a specific subject, a specific doubt, or your current mock score breakdown — I can help much more precisely.
